memberdef.definition: typedef int(* json_append_bytes_t) (const char *bytes, size_t len, void *data)
memberdef.name: json_append_bytes_t

briefdescription.para: Function pointer type to append bytes to a buffer while encoding JSON data.

parameterdescription.para: Contents to write to the output
Number of bytes to append to output
User-provided pointer

simplesect.para: This callback function should return a negative number on error (which will be propagated to the return value of json_obj_encode()), or 0 on success.

memberdef.argsstring: (char *json, size_t len, const struct json_obj_descr *descr, size_t descr_len, void *val)
memberdef.name: json_obj_parse

briefdescription.para: Parses the JSON-encoded object pointed to by json, with size len, according to the descriptor pointed to by descr.

detaileddescription.para: Values are stored in a struct pointed to by val. Set up the descriptor like this:
struct s { int32_t foo; char *bar; } struct json_obj_descr descr[] = { JSON_OBJ_DESCR_PRIM(struct s, foo, JSON_TOK_NUMBER), JSON_OBJ_DESCR_PRIM(struct s, bar, JSON_TOK_STRING), };

Since this parser is designed for machine-to-machine communications, some liberties were taken to simplify the design: (1) strings are not unescaped (but only valid escape sequences are accepted); (2) no UTF-8 validation is performed; and (3) only integer numbers are supported (no strtod() in the minimal libc).

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to JSON-encoded value to be parsed
Length of JSON-encoded value
Pointer to the descriptor array
Number of elements in the descriptor array. Must be less than 63 due to implementation detail reasons (if more fields are necessary, use two descriptors)
Pointer to the struct to hold the decoded values

simplesect.para: < 0 if error, bitmap of decoded fields on success (bit 0 is set if first field in the descriptor has been properly decoded, etc).

memberdef.argsstring: (char *json, size_t len, const struct json_obj_descr *descr, void *val)
memberdef.name: json_arr_parse

briefdescription.para: Parses the JSON-encoded array pointed to by json, with size len, according to the descriptor pointed to by descr.

detaileddescription.para: Values are stored in a struct pointed to by val. Set up the descriptor like this:

struct s { int32_t foo; char *bar; } struct json_obj_descr descr[] = { JSON_OBJ_DESCR_PRIM(struct s, foo, JSON_TOK_NUMBER), JSON_OBJ_DESCR_PRIM(struct s, bar, JSON_TOK_STRING), }; struct a { struct s baz[10]; size_t count; } struct json_obj_descr array[] = { JSON_OBJ_DESCR_OBJ_ARRAY(struct a, baz, 10, count, descr, ARRAY_SIZE(descr)), };

Since this parser is designed for machine-to-machine communications, some liberties were taken to simplify the design: (1) strings are not unescaped (but only valid escape sequences are accepted); (2) no UTF-8 validation is performed; and (3) only integer numbers are supported (no strtod() in the minimal libc).

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to JSON-encoded array to be parsed
Length of JSON-encoded array
Pointer to the descriptor array
Pointer to the struct to hold the decoded values

simplesect.para: 0 if array has been successfully parsed. A negative value indicates an error (as defined on errno.h).

briefdescription.para: Initialize single-object array parsing.

detaileddescription.para: JSON-encoded array data is going to be parsed one object at a time. Data is provided by payload with the size of len bytes.
Function validate that Json Array start is detected and initialize json object for Json object parsing separately.

parameterdescription.para: Provide storage for parser states. To be used when parsing the array.
Pointer to JSON-encoded array to be parsed
Length of JSON-encoded array

simplesect.para: 0 if array start is detected and initialization is successful or negative error code in case of failure.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct json_obj *json, const struct json_obj_descr *descr, size_t descr_len, void *val)
memberdef.name: json_arr_separate_parse_object

briefdescription.para: Parse a single object from array.

detaileddescription.para: Parses the JSON-encoded object pointed to by json object array, with size len, according to the descriptor pointed to by descr.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to JSON-object message state
Pointer to the descriptor array
Number of elements in the descriptor array. Must be less than 31.
Pointer to the struct to hold the decoded values

simplesect.para: < 0 if error, 0 for end of message, bitmap of decoded fields on success (bit 0 is set if first field in the descriptor has been properly decoded, etc).

briefdescription.para: Escapes the string so it can be used to encode JSON objects.

parameterdescription.para: The string to escape; the escape string is stored the buffer pointed to by this parameter
Points to a size_t containing the size before and after the escaping process
The size of buffer str points to

simplesect.para: 0 if string has been escaped properly, or -ENOMEM if there was not enough space to escape the buffer

briefdescription.para: Calculates the JSON-escaped string length.

parameterdescription.para: The string to analyze
String size

simplesect.para: The length str would have if it were escaped

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct json_obj_descr *descr, size_t descr_len, const void *val)
memberdef.name: json_calc_encoded_len

briefdescription.para: Calculates the string length to fully encode an object.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the descriptor array
Number of elements in the descriptor array
Struct holding the values

simplesect.para: Number of bytes necessary to encode the values if >0, an error code is returned.

briefdescription.para: Calculates the string length to fully encode an array.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the descriptor array
Struct holding the values

simplesect.para: Number of bytes necessary to encode the values if >0, an error code is returned.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct json_obj_descr *descr, size_t descr_len, const void *val, char *buffer, size_t buf_size)
memberdef.name: json_obj_encode_buf

briefdescription.para: Encodes an object in a contiguous memory location.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the descriptor array
Number of elements in the descriptor array
Struct holding the values
Buffer to store the JSON data
Size of buffer, in bytes, with space for the terminating NUL character

simplesect.para: 0 if object has been successfully encoded. A negative value indicates an error (as defined on errno.h).

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct json_obj_descr *descr, const void *val, char *buffer, size_t buf_size)
memberdef.name: json_arr_encode_buf

briefdescription.para: Encodes an array in a contiguous memory location.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the descriptor array
Struct holding the values
Buffer to store the JSON data
Size of buffer, in bytes, with space for the terminating NUL character

simplesect.para: 0 if object has been successfully encoded. A negative value indicates an error (as defined on errno.h).

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct json_obj_descr *descr, size_t descr_len, const void *val, json_append_bytes_t append_bytes, void *data)
memberdef.name: json_obj_encode

briefdescription.para: Encodes an object using an arbitrary writer function.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the descriptor array
Number of elements in the descriptor array
Struct holding the values
Function to append bytes to the output
Data pointer to be passed to the append_bytes callback function.

simplesect.para: 0 if object has been successfully encoded. A negative value indicates an error.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct json_obj_descr *descr, const void *val, json_append_bytes_t append_bytes, void *data)
memberdef.name: json_arr_encode

briefdescription.para: Encodes an array using an arbitrary writer function.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the descriptor array
Struct holding the values
Function to append bytes to the output
Data pointer to be passed to the append_bytes callback function.

simplesect.para: 0 if object has been successfully encoded. A negative value indicates an error.

memberdef.argsstring: (char *json, size_t len, const struct json_mixed_arr_descr *descr, size_t descr_len, void *val)
memberdef.name: json_mixed_arr_parse

briefdescription.para: Parse a JSON mixed array into a C structure.

detaileddescription.para: This function parses a JSON array (which may contain elements of varying types) according to the provided mixed array descriptor and stores the result in the user-supplied structure.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the input JSON string.
Length of the input JSON string.
Descriptor array describing the structure of the mixed array.
Number of elements in the descriptor array.
Pointer to the structure to populate with parsed data.

simplesect.para: < 0 if error, number of elements parsed on success.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct json_mixed_arr_descr *descr, size_t descr_len, void *val, json_append_bytes_t append_bytes, void *data)
memberdef.name: json_mixed_arr_encode

briefdescription.para: Encode a C structure as a JSON mixed array.

detaileddescription.para: This function encodes a C structure, described by the mixed array descriptor, as a JSON array.

parameterdescription.para: Descriptor array describing the structure of the mixed array.
Number of elements in the descriptor array.
Pointer to the structure to encode.
Function to append bytes to the output
Data pointer to be passed to the append_bytes callback function.

simplesect.para: 0 if mixed array has been successfully encoded. Negative error code on failure.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct json_mixed_arr_descr *descr, size_t descr_len, void *val, char *buffer, size_t buf_size)
memberdef.name: json_mixed_arr_encode_buf

briefdescription.para: Encode a C structure as a JSON mixed array into a buffer.

detaileddescription.para: This function encodes a C structure, described by the mixed array descriptor, as a JSON array and writes the result into the provided buffer.

parameterdescription.para: Descriptor array describing the structure of the mixed array.
Number of elements in the descriptor array.
Pointer to the structure to encode.
Output buffer to write the JSON string.
Size of the output buffer.

simplesect.para: 0 if mixed array has been successfully encoded. Negative error code on failure.

briefdescription.para: Calculate the length of the encoded JSON mixed array.

detaileddescription.para: This function calculates the number of bytes required to encode the given structure as a JSON mixed array, according to the provided descriptor.

parameterdescription.para: Descriptor array describing the structure of the mixed array.
Number of elements in the descriptor array.
Pointer to the structure to encode.

simplesect.para: Number of bytes required for encoding, or negative error code.

memberdef.name: JSON_OBJ_DESCR_PRIM
memberdef.initializer: { \ .field_name = (#field_name_), \ .align_shift = Z_ALIGN_SHIFT(struct_), \ .field_name_len = sizeof(#field_name_) - 1, \ .type = type_, \ .offset = offsetof(struct_, field_name_), \ .field = { \ .size = SIZEOF_FIELD(struct_, field_name_) \ }, \ }

briefdescription.para: Helper macro to declare a descriptor for supported primitive values.

memberdef.name: JSON_OBJ_DESCR_OBJECT
memberdef.initializer: { \ .field_name = (#field_name_), \ .align_shift = Z_ALIGN_SHIFT(struct_), \ .field_name_len = (sizeof(#field_name_) - 1), \ .type = JSON_TOK_OBJECT_START, \ .offset = offsetof(struct_, field_name_), \ .object = { \ .sub_descr = sub_descr_, \ .sub_descr_len = ARRAY_SIZE(sub_descr_), \ }, \ }

briefdescription.para: Helper macro to declare a descriptor for an object value.

memberdef.name: JSON_OBJ_DESCR_ARRAY
memberdef.initializer: { \ .field_name = (#field_name_), \ .align_shift = Z_ALIGN_SHIFT(struct_), \ .field_name_len = sizeof(#field_name_) - 1, \ .type = JSON_TOK_ARRAY_START, \ .offset = offsetof(struct_, field_name_), \ .array = { \ .element_descr = Z_JSON_ELEMENT_DESCR(struct_, len_field_, \ elem_type_, \ Z_JSON_DESCR_FIELD(struct_, field_name_[0])), \ .n_elements = (max_len_), \ }, \ }

briefdescription.para: Helper macro to declare a descriptor for an array of primitives.

memberdef.name: JSON_OBJ_DESCR_OBJ_ARRAY
memberdef.initializer: { \ .field_name = (#field_name_), \ .align_shift = Z_ALIGN_SHIFT(struct_), \ .field_name_len = sizeof(#field_name_) - 1, \ .type = JSON_TOK_ARRAY_START, \ .offset = offsetof(struct_, field_name_), \ .array = { \ .element_descr = Z_JSON_ELEMENT_DESCR(struct_, len_field_, \ JSON_TOK_OBJECT_START, \ Z_JSON_DESCR_OBJ(elem_descr_, elem_descr_len_)), \ .n_elements = (max_len_), \ }, \ }

briefdescription.para: Helper macro to declare a descriptor for an array of objects.

memberdef.name: JSON_OBJ_DESCR_ARRAY_ARRAY
memberdef.initializer: { \ .field_name = (#field_name_), \ .align_shift = Z_ALIGN_SHIFT(struct_), \ .field_name_len = sizeof(#field_name_) - 1, \ .type = JSON_TOK_ARRAY_START, \ .offset = offsetof(struct_, field_name_), \ .array = { \ .element_descr = Z_JSON_ELEMENT_DESCR( \ struct_, len_field_, JSON_TOK_ARRAY_START, \ Z_JSON_DESCR_ARRAY( \ elem_descr_, \ 1 + ZERO_OR_COMPILE_ERROR(elem_descr_len_ == 1))), \ .n_elements = (max_len_), \ }, \ }

briefdescription.para: Helper macro to declare a descriptor for an array of array.

memberdef.name: JSON_OBJ_DESCR_ARRAY_ARRAY_NAMED
memberdef.initializer: { \ .field_name = (#json_field_name_), \ .align_shift = Z_ALIGN_SHIFT(struct_), \ .field_name_len = sizeof(#json_field_name_) - 1, \ .type = JSON_TOK_ARRAY_START, \ .offset = offsetof(struct_, struct_field_name_), \ .array = { \ .element_descr = Z_JSON_ELEMENT_DESCR( \ struct_, len_field_, JSON_TOK_ARRAY_START, \ Z_JSON_DESCR_ARRAY( \ elem_descr_, \ 1 + ZERO_OR_COMPILE_ERROR(elem_descr_len_ == 1))), \ .n_elements = (max_len_), \ }, \ }

briefdescription.para: Variant of JSON_OBJ_DESCR_ARRAY_ARRAY that can be used when the structure and JSON field names differ.

detaileddescription.para: This is useful when the JSON field is not a valid C identifier.

parameterdescription.para: Struct packing the values
String, field name in JSON strings
Field name in the struct containing the array
Maximum number of elements in the array
Field name in the struct for the number of elements in the array
Element descriptor, pointer to a descriptor array
Number of elements in elem_descr_

memberdef.name: JSON_OBJ_DESCR_PRIM_NAMED
memberdef.initializer: { \ .field_name = (json_field_name_), \ .align_shift = Z_ALIGN_SHIFT(struct_), \ .field_name_len = sizeof(json_field_name_) - 1, \ .type = type_, \ .offset = offsetof(struct_, struct_field_name_), \ .field = { \ .size = SIZEOF_FIELD(struct_, struct_field_name_) \ }, \ }

briefdescription.para: Variant of JSON_OBJ_DESCR_PRIM that can be used when the structure and JSON field names differ.

detaileddescription.para: This is useful when the JSON field is not a valid C identifier.

parameterdescription.para: Struct packing the values.
String, field name in JSON strings
Field name in the struct
Token type for JSON value corresponding to a primitive type.

memberdef.name: JSON_OBJ_DESCR_OBJECT_NAMED
memberdef.initializer: { \ .field_name = (json_field_name_), \ .align_shift = Z_ALIGN_SHIFT(struct_), \ .field_name_len = (sizeof(json_field_name_) - 1), \ .type = JSON_TOK_OBJECT_START, \ .offset = offsetof(struct_, struct_field_name_), \ .object = { \ .sub_descr = sub_descr_, \ .sub_descr_len = ARRAY_SIZE(sub_descr_), \ }, \ }

briefdescription.para: Variant of JSON_OBJ_DESCR_OBJECT that can be used when the structure and JSON field names differ.

detaileddescription.para: This is useful when the JSON field is not a valid C identifier.

parameterdescription.para: Struct packing the values
String, field name in JSON strings
Field name in the struct
Array of json_obj_descr describing the subobject

memberdef.name: JSON_OBJ_DESCR_ARRAY_NAMED
memberdef.initializer: { \ .field_name = (json_field_name_), \ .align_shift = Z_ALIGN_SHIFT(struct_), \ .field_name_len = sizeof(json_field_name_) - 1, \ .type = JSON_TOK_ARRAY_START, \ .offset = offsetof(struct_, struct_field_name_), \ .array = { \ .element_descr = Z_JSON_ELEMENT_DESCR(struct_, len_field_, \ elem_type_, \ Z_JSON_DESCR_FIELD(struct_, struct_field_name_[0])), \ .n_elements = (max_len_), \ }, \ }

briefdescription.para: Variant of JSON_OBJ_DESCR_ARRAY that can be used when the structure and JSON field names differ.

detaileddescription.para: This is useful when the JSON field is not a valid C identifier.

parameterdescription.para: Struct packing the values
String, field name in JSON strings
Field name in the struct
Maximum number of elements in array
Field name in the struct for the number of elements in the array
Element type, must be a primitive type

memberdef.name: JSON_OBJ_DESCR_OBJ_ARRAY_NAMED
memberdef.initializer: { \ .field_name = json_field_name_, \ .align_shift = Z_ALIGN_SHIFT(struct_), \ .field_name_len = sizeof(json_field_name_) - 1, \ .type = JSON_TOK_ARRAY_START, \ .offset = offsetof(struct_, struct_field_name_), \ .array = { \ .element_descr = Z_JSON_ELEMENT_DESCR(struct_, len_field_, \ JSON_TOK_OBJECT_START, \ Z_JSON_DESCR_OBJ(elem_descr_, elem_descr_len_)), \ .n_elements = (max_len_), \ }, \ }

briefdescription.para: Variant of JSON_OBJ_DESCR_OBJ_ARRAY that can be used when the structure and JSON field names differ.

detaileddescription.para: This is useful when the JSON field is not a valid C identifier.

memberdef.name: JSON_MIXED_ARR_DESCR_PRIM
memberdef.initializer: { \ .type = type_, \ .count_offset = offsetof(struct_, count_field_), \ .primitive = { \ .offset = offsetof(struct_, field_name_), \ .size = SIZEOF_FIELD(struct_, field_name_) \ } \ }

briefdescription.para: Helper macro to declare a mixed array primitive element descriptor.

detaileddescription.para:
struct_
Struct containing the value.
field_name_
Field name in the struct.
type_
Token type for the JSON value (see enum json_tokens).
count_field_
Field name in the struct for the number of elements.
Example: struct foo { int x; size_t x_count; }; const struct json_mixed_arr_descr foo_descr[] = { JSON_MIXED_ARR_DESCR_PRIM(struct foo, x, JSON_TOK_NUMBER, x_count), };

memberdef.name: JSON_MIXED_ARR_DESCR_OBJECT
memberdef.initializer: { \ .type = JSON_TOK_OBJECT_START, \ .count_offset = offsetof(struct_, count_field_), \ .object = { \ .sub_descr = sub_descr_, \ .sub_descr_len = ARRAY_SIZE(sub_descr_), \ .offset = offsetof(struct_, field_name_) \ } \ }

briefdescription.para: Helper macro to declare a mixed array object element descriptor.

detaileddescription.para:
struct_
Struct containing the object.
field_name_
Field name in the struct.
sub_descr_
Array of json_obj_descr describing the object fields.
count_field_
Field name in the struct for the number of elements.
Example: struct bar { int y; }; struct foo { struct bar b; size_t b_count; }; const struct json_obj_descr bar_descr[] = { ... }; const struct json_mixed_arr_descr foo_descr[] = { JSON_MIXED_ARR_DESCR_OBJECT(struct foo, b, bar_descr, b_count), };

memberdef.name: JSON_MIXED_ARR_DESCR_ARRAY
memberdef.initializer: { \ .type = JSON_TOK_ARRAY_START, \ .count_offset = offsetof(struct_, count_field_), \ .array = { \ .element_descr = elem_descr_, \ .n_elements = (max_len_), \ .offset = offsetof(struct_, field_name_) \ } \ }

briefdescription.para: Helper macro to declare a mixed array homogeneous array element descriptor.

detaileddescription.para:
struct_
Struct containing the array.
field_name_
Field name in the struct.
max_len_
Maximum number of elements in the array.
elem_descr_
Element descriptor (pointer to json_obj_descr array).
count_field_
Field name in the struct for the number of elements.
Example: struct foo { int arr[4]; size_t arr_count; }; const struct json_obj_descr arr_elem_descr[] = { JSON_OBJ_DESCR_ARRAY(struct foo, arr, 4, arr_count, JSON_TOK_NUMBER), }; const struct json_mixed_arr_descr foo_descr[] = { JSON_MIXED_ARR_DESCR_ARRAY(struct foo, arr, 4, arr_elem_descr, arr_count), };

memberdef.name: JSON_MIXED_ARR_DESCR_MIXED_ARRAY
memberdef.initializer: { \ .type = JSON_TOK_MIXED_ARRAY, \ .count_offset = offsetof(struct_, count_field_), \ .mixed_array = { \ .sub_descr = sub_descr_, \ .sub_descr_len = ARRAY_SIZE(sub_descr_), \ .offset = offsetof(struct_, field_name_) \ } \ }

briefdescription.para: Helper macro to declare a nested mixed array element descriptor.

detaileddescription.para:
struct_
Struct containing the nested mixed array.
field_name_
Field name in the struct.
sub_descr_
Mixed array descriptor for the nested array.
count_field_
Field name in the struct for the number of elements.
Example: struct foo { ...; size_t nested_count; }; const struct json_mixed_arr_descr nested_descr[] = { ... }; const struct json_mixed_arr_descr foo_descr[] = { JSON_MIXED_ARR_DESCR_MIXED_ARRAY(struct foo, nested, nested_descr, nested_count), };
